content.claim: Hungary had the highest level of segregation of students from less and more advantaged socioeconomic groups into different schools in the EU, with the highest segregation of the most and least disadvantaged students in the 2022 PISA study
content.context: Fresh live research through the Polish system reform lens
limitations: Identifies between-school segregation as a pattern but does not establish whether the 6/8-year track is the primary driver or one mechanism among several
Structural factors such as neighbourhood segregation and long-standing admission policies are cited by CEU analysis as likely primary drivers
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
